terms.definition: A unique identifier assigned to one training run; the primary key for retrieving that run's parameters, metrics, tags, and artifacts.
Synonym for experiment ID in most tracking tools—one ID per execution of a training or evaluation job.
A user-defined label on a run (e.g. baseline, candidate) used for filtering and grouping; not guaranteed unique across runs.
A catalog of named, versioned models with stages and metadata, each version linked to a source run and artifact path.
One numbered entry in the registry (e.g. fraud-detector v5) pointing at a specific artifact from a specific run ID.
The trace from a deployed model back through registry version, source run ID, params, data, and code that produced it.
